description: PREDICT -> OBSERVE -> EXPLAIN -> RECAP thinking tool for NCERT Class 6 Science Chapter 8 (A Journey through States of Water). A learner predicts what happens to an ice cube, then scrubs or plays a 0-10 minute melt animation (a chunky cube floats and shrinks in a wide cup while a shallow pool grows), sees the concept reveal (solid -> liquid = melting), and compares the result with their prediction. A Practice mode adds a multiple-choice quiz whose answer positions are shuffled (no positional pattern). Fully responsive (stacks on small screens). Styled with the Singularity design system (Poppins, indigo #4A4DC9, pill buttons).
subject: Science
chapter: 8 - A Journey through States of Water

width.description: Max width of the tool in px (it is fluid up to this)

height.description: Reference height; layout is responsive and height is auto

autoPlayDuration.description: Auto-advance is disabled for this interactive tool

totalMinutes.description: Simulated minutes from full ice to fully melted

meltDurationMs.description: Real-time ms for one full auto-melt (divided by animationSpeed)

showPrediction.description: Lock the experiment until the learner makes a prediction

predictionOptions.description: Override the predict-scene choices; exactly one should have correct:true

default.predict: Socho! Cup ne table par mukshu to ice nu shu thashe? (Predict before you observe)
default.observe: Dhyaan thi juo - ice motu thay chhe ke nanu? Pani kya thi aavyu?
default.explain: Ice gayab nathi thayu... ae pani ma convert thayu. Aa process ne 'melting' kahevay.
default.recap: Tamari prediction sachi hati ke nahi? Compare karo!

conceptLines.description: Lines revealed one-by-one in the Explain scene
default: Ice is water in the SOLID state.
Water is the same substance in the LIQUID state.
On warming, solid -> liquid. This process is called MELTING.
Nothing was added or lost - it is the same water!

quiz.description: Practice-mode questions. Option order is automatically shuffled at runtime so the correct answer has no positional pattern; answerIndex refers to the order as written here.

default.question: When ice changes into water, this process is called...
default.explanation: Solid to liquid is melting. Liquid to solid is freezing.

default.question: Ice and water are...
options: The same substance in different states
default.explanation: Ice and water are the same substance - just solid and liquid states.

default.question: To turn water back into ice, we should...
default.explanation: Cooling liquid water in a freezer makes it freeze into ice.

default.question: What makes an ice cube melt when left on a table?
options: It loses heat to the room
It gains heat from the warm room
Nothing - it melts on its own
default.explanation: The warm surroundings supply heat to the ice, so it melts into water.

default.question: Which property is TRUE for ice (solid state)?
options: It flows and takes the shape of the container
It keeps its own fixed shape
It spreads to fill all space
default.explanation: A solid keeps its shape. A liquid takes the container's shape; a gas fills all space.

default.question: A puddle of water dries up in the sun. The water has...
default.explanation: Liquid water turning into vapour is evaporation - it speeds up in sunlight.

default.question: As the ice cube melts, what happens to the amount of water in the cup?
default.explanation: The melting ice turns into liquid water, so the water level rises.

modes.learn: Guided 4-scene flow: Predict -> Observe -> Explain -> Recap
modes.practice: Multiple-choice quick check with shuffled answers, instant feedback, and scoring (no animation/stage shown)

example_tool_call.title: Melting Ice Cube
example_tool_call.instructions_for_student: First predict what will happen, then watch the ice melt!
